title: Meta_Variant
text: Meta_Variant Identifies the meta variant character set for which the current translation table is intended. It ensures that Uniface meta character definitions used in translation table definitions prior to Uniface 9 are automatically mapped to Unicode characters. meta_ Variant TableType Arguments Variant —generic name for a character set, which may be implemented by one ore more platform-specific characters sets or code pages. TableType —mnemonic specifying the type of translation table; one of: ^display —identifies a device translation table for a display device ^printer —identifies a device translation table for a printer device ^keyboard —identifies a keyboard translation table for a keyboard ^dbms —identifies a keyboard translation table for a database If omitted, the current meta variant of the Uniface IDE is used for the mapping. Allowed Meta Variants meta_ Variant Description External Character Set Unicode Unicode UTF8 A subset is used for meta_ Variant meta_0 meta_Western Western LATIN! (DEC) CP437 (IBMPC, PC) CP850 AIX (IBMRT) EBCDIC meta_1 meta_Japanese Japanese SJIS EUC meta_2 meta_Simplified_Chinese Simplified Chinese GB (GB2312) meta_3 meta_Traditional_Chinese Traditional Chinese (Traditional Chinese character sets are only partially implemented.) BIG5 CNS (CNS11643) IBM5550 ETEN meta_4 meta_Korean Korean KSC (KSC5601) meta_5 meta_Eastern_Europe Eastern European CP1250 LATIN2 meta_6 meta_Cyrillic Cyrillic CP1251 LATIN5 meta_7 meta_Greek Greek CP1253 LATIN7 meta_8 meta_Hebrew Hebrew CP1255 LATIN8 meta_9 meta_Arabic Arabic CP1256 LATIN6 ARAIX Description Meta variants are internal character sets that are mapped to external character sets independently of the platform, for example meta_Western to identify character sets used for Western languages such as English, German, and Spanish. The meta_ Variant mnemonic ensures that Uniface meta character definitions (Font 0 through 7) used in translation table definitions prior to Uniface 9 are automatically mapped to Unicode characters. Uniface now uses Unicode as its internal character set rather than the meta character sets. It is also possible to create character set mappings in the [META_CharSet] sections of the assignment file. Related Links Meta Variants A meta variant is an internal character set that is used to translate between Unicode and a supported external character set. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
